name: d.school Micro-Cycle
description: Stanford d.school micro-cycle: Understand/Observe (diverge) -> Define POV (converge) -> Ideate (diverge) -> Prototype & Test (converge).
when_to_use: Human-centered design when you want empathy-first divergence and rapid prototype testing.

phases.name: Understand & Observe
phases.intent: Build empathy: observe and surface user needs, contexts and frictions broadly.

phases.name: Define POV
phases.intent: Consolidate observations into a single Point-of-View statement (user + need + insight).

phases.name: Ideate
phases.intent: Generate many diverse solution concepts; build a prototype for the most promising.

phases.name: Prototype & Test
phases.intent: Personas use the prototype; gather feedback and converge to refined requirements.
